definition: The maximum number of tokens (words/characters) a model can process in a single request, including both input and output. A larger context window lets you feed in longer documents, conversations, or codebases at once.

The maximum amount of API usage allowed in a given time period. It may be measured as requests per minute (RPM), tokens per minute (TPM), or requests per day (RPD). Exceeding limits causes requests to be temporarily rejected.
Requests Per Minute — the maximum number of API calls you can make in a 60-second window. Exceeding this limit results in rate-limit errors until the window resets.
Tokens Per Minute — the maximum number of tokens (input + output combined) that can be processed per minute. This limits the total throughput of text processed by the API.
Requests Per Day — the maximum number of API calls allowed over a 24-hour period. A daily cap in addition to per-minute limits.
The cost charged for the text you send to the model (your prompt, documents, conversation history). Measured in dollars per 1 million tokens.
The cost charged for the text the model generates in response. Usually higher than input price. Measured in dollars per 1 million tokens.
The basic unit of text that AI models process. Roughly 4 characters or 0.75 words in English. A page of text is approximately 500 tokens. Both input and output are measured in tokens.
A capability that lets the model call predefined functions or tools, enabling it to interact with external systems, APIs, or databases. Essential for building AI agents.
Extended chain-of-thought processing where the model thinks through problems step-by-step before giving a final answer. Improves accuracy on math, science, and logic tasks.
Mixture of Experts — a model architecture where only a subset of parameters are activated for each input. This allows very large total parameter counts while keeping inference costs manageable.

Retrieval-Augmented Generation — a technique where relevant documents are retrieved from a knowledge base and included in the prompt, allowing the model to answer questions about private or up-to-date information.
